description: Drinking water at Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune and MCAS New River, North Carolina was contaminated with volatile organic compounds (TCE, PCE, vinyl chloride, benzene). ATSDR identified TCE peaking at 1,400 ppb at Hadnot Point and PCE at 215 ppb at Tarawa Terrace. Veterans, reservists, and National Guard members who served at least 30 days total between Aug 1, 1953 and Dec 31, 1987 are presumed exposed.
presumptive_authority: Camp Lejeune Justice Act of 2022 (PACT Act 2022, Pub.L.117-168 §804); 38 CFR 3.307

presumptive_conditions.name: Aplastic anemia and other myelodysplastic syndromes

documentation_requirements.why: Establishes service period and duty station
Proves 30+ cumulative days at Camp Lejeune or MCAS New River
Confirms one of the 8 presumptive conditions

notes: Hadnot Point VOC exceedance Aug 1953-Jan 1985; Tarawa Terrace PCE exceedance Nov 1957-Feb 1987. Minimum 30 cumulative days service required.

exposure_name: Agent Orange / Tactical Herbicides - Republic of Vietnam
description: Tactical herbicides including 2,4-D, 2,4,5-T (with contaminant TCDD dioxin), cacodylic acid, and picloram were sprayed in Vietnam. Service in the Republic of Vietnam, on inland waterways, or within 12 nautical miles of the Vietnam/Cambodia coast between Jan 9, 1962 and May 7, 1975 is presumptive for herbicide exposure.
presumptive_authority: 38 CFR 3.307(a)(6); 38 CFR 3.309(e); Agent Orange Act of 1991; Blue Water Navy Vietnam Veterans Act 2019 (Pub.L.116-23); PACT Act 2022 (Pub.L.117-168)

documentation_requirements.why: Establishes service in Vietnam during qualifying period
Confirms boots-on-ground or qualifying offshore service

notes: 12 nautical mile (Blue Water Navy) coverage added by Pub.L.116-23.

exposure_name: Agent Orange - U.S. or Royal Thai Military Bases (Thailand)
description: Service at U.S. or Royal Thai military bases in Thailand between Jan 9, 1962 and Jun 30, 1976 is presumptive for herbicide exposure under the PACT Act.
notes: PACT Act removed the prior perimeter-duty requirement.

description: Service in Laos between Dec 1, 1965 and Sep 30, 1969 is presumptive for herbicide exposure under the PACT Act.
notes: Full 38 CFR 3.309(e) disease list applies; abbreviated above for brevity.

exposure_name: Agent Orange - Cambodia (Mimot / Krek, Kampong Cham Province)
description: Service at Mimot or Krek in Kampong Cham Province, Cambodia, from Apr 16, 1969 through Apr 30, 1969 is presumptive for herbicide exposure under the PACT Act.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms presence in Mimot or Krek

notes: Very narrow geographic and date window.

exposure_name: Agent Orange - Guam / American Samoa (and territorial waters)
description: Service on Guam, American Samoa, or in their territorial waters between Jan 9, 1962 and Jul 31, 1980 is presumptive for herbicide exposure under the PACT Act.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms Guam or American Samoa duty

description: Service on Johnston Atoll, or aboard a ship that called at Johnston Atoll, between Jan 1, 1972 and Sep 30, 1977 is presumptive for herbicide exposure under the PACT Act. Surplus Agent Orange was stored and later incinerated at Johnston Atoll.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms presence on/calling at Johnston Atoll

notes: Includes ships that called at Johnston Atoll.

description: Service in or near the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) between Sep 1, 1967 and Aug 31, 1971 is presumptive for herbicide exposure.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms DMZ unit during qualifying window

notes: CFR-narrower variant: Apr 1, 1968 - Aug 31, 1971; VA-published broader window preserved here.

description: Air Force and Air Force Reserve flight, ground maintenance, and aeromedical evacuation crew members who regularly and repeatedly operated, maintained, or served onboard the same C-123 aircraft used for Operation Ranch Hand spraying are presumed exposed to Agent Orange residue.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms duty AFSC and unit assignment on C-123
Documents regular contact with contaminated C-123

notes: Established by VA final rule 80 Fed. Reg. 35246 (Jun 19, 2015), codified at 38 CFR 3.307(a)(6)(v).

exposure_name: Burn Pits and Airborne Hazards - Post-9/11 Theater
description: Open-air burn pits and fine particulate matter exposure during post-9/11 service in Afghanistan, Djibouti, Egypt, Jordan, Lebanon, Syria, Uzbekistan, Yemen, and the airspace above.

documentation_requirements.why: Establishes service on or after Sep 11, 2001
Confirms service in qualifying country or airspace

documentation_requirements.type: Airborne Hazards and Open Burn Pit Registry record

notes: No end date - presumption continues for ongoing/future service in listed locations.

exposure_name: Burn Pits and Airborne Hazards - Gulf War Theater
description: Open-air burn pits and fine particulate matter exposure during Gulf War service in Bahrain, Iraq, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Somalia, the United Arab Emirates, and surrounding waters plus the neutral zone between Iraq and Saudi Arabia.

documentation_requirements.why: Establishes Gulf War-era service on/after Aug 2, 1990
Confirms qualifying country or maritime zone

exposure_name: Gulf War Illness (Southwest Asia) - Undiagnosed Illness & MUCMI
description: Veterans of Southwest Asia theater (on or after Aug 2, 1990) with chronic undiagnosed illness or MUCMI of 6+ months duration may receive a presumption of service connection.

presumptive_conditions.name: Undiagnosed illness with cardiovascular, muscle/joint, headache, neurological, neuropsychological, skin, respiratory, sleep, GI, or urological signs

documentation_requirements.why: Required for MUCMI / undiagnosed illness presumption
documentation_requirements.type: Medical records showing 6+ months symptom duration

description: Veterans who served in Afghanistan after Sep 19, 2001 are presumed service-connected for certain infectious diseases. Diagnosis generally required within 1 year of separation (tuberculosis and visceral leishmaniasis have no time limit).

documentation_requirements.type: Medical diagnosis within 1 year of separation

notes: Airspace excluded.

exposure_name: Ionizing Radiation - Atomic Veterans (Atmospheric Nuclear Tests)
description: Veterans who participated in atmospheric nuclear weapons tests conducted by the U.S. primarily in Nevada and the Pacific Ocean between 1945 and 1962 are recognized as atomic veterans under 38 CFR 3.309(d).
presumptive_authority: 38 CFR 3.309(d); Radiation Exposed Veterans Compensation Act of 1988 (Pub.L.100-321)

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms participation in atmospheric nuclear test

notes: Trinity test (Jul 16, 1945) through last US atmospheric test in Dominic series (Nov 4, 1962).

exposure_name: Ionizing Radiation - American Occupation of Hiroshima or Nagasaki
description: Veterans who participated in the American occupation of Hiroshima or Nagasaki, Japan, between Aug 6, 1945 and Jul 1, 1946 are presumed exposed to ionizing radiation.
presumptive_authority: 38 CFR 3.309(d)(3)(ii)(B); Radiation Exposed Veterans Compensation Act of 1988

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms occupation duty in Hiroshima or Nagasaki

notes: POW status near these cities also qualifies.

description: Service in the cleanup of Enewetak Atoll, Marshall Islands, from Jan 1, 1977 through Dec 31, 1980 is presumptive for ionizing radiation exposure.

presumptive_conditions.name: Colon, stomach, esophagus, small intestine, pancreas cancers

notes: Added as a PACT Act radiation-risk activity expansion.

exposure_name: Ionizing Radiation - Palomares, Spain Plutonium Cleanup
description: Service responding to the Jan 17, 1966 B-52/KC-135 mid-air collision over Palomares, Spain, which released plutonium from broken hydrogen bombs.
notes: PACT Act addition.

exposure_name: Ionizing Radiation - Thule Air Force Base, Greenland
description: Service in response to the Jan 21, 1968 B-52 bomber crash and fire near Thule Air Force Base, Greenland.
notes: PACT Act addition.

exposure_name: Ionizing Radiation - Amchitka Island, Alaska Underground Nuclear Testing
description: Service on or before Jan 1, 1974 in conjunction with underground nuclear testing at Amchitka Island, Alaska (Long Shot 1965, Milrow 1969, Cannikin 1971).
presumptive_authority: 38 CFR 3.309(d)(3)(ii); Radiation Exposed Veterans Compensation Act of 1988
notes: Three named tests: Long Shot (1965), Milrow (1969), Cannikin (1971).

exposure_name: Ionizing Radiation - Gaseous Diffusion Plants (Paducah, Portsmouth, K-25)
description: Service of at least 250 days before Feb 1, 1992 at Paducah (KY), Portsmouth (OH), or K-25 area of Oak Ridge (TN) Gaseous Diffusion Plants.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms 250+ cumulative days at qualifying plant

notes: Minimum 250 cumulative days required.

exposure_name: Mustard Gas / Lewisite - WWII Full-Body Chemical Testing
description: Service members who participated in WWII-era full-body exposure to nitrogen mustard, sulfur mustard, or Lewisite during U.S. military chemical warfare testing (Dec 7, 1941 - Dec 31, 1946) are presumed service-connected for specific conditions per 38 CFR 3.316.

presumptive_conditions.name: Squamous cell carcinoma of the skin

documentation_requirements.why: Documents participation in chemical testing program

documentation_requirements.type: DoD or VA confirmation of testing

notes: Acute nonlymphocytic leukemia presumption applies only to nitrogen mustard exposure.

exposure_name: Project 112 / Project SHAD - DoD Chem-Bio Warfare Testing
description: Project 112 was a series of DoD-conducted chemical and biological warfare tests from 1962 to 1974. Project SHAD was the at-sea component. Claims adjudicated case-by-case.
presumptive_authority: VA recognition; DoD declassified fact sheets

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms participation in specific test event

notes: No published presumption list; claims adjudicated case-by-case.

description: U.S. military occupied K2 Air Base in Uzbekistan from 2001 to 2005. Documented hazards include leaking Soviet-era jet fuel (VOCs), low-level depleted uranium contamination, particulate matter, asbestos, and lead-based paint. Uzbekistan is among the PACT Act post-9/11 presumptive locations.

presumptive_conditions.name: All other PACT Act post-9/11 burn-pit presumptive cancers and respiratory conditions

description: During the 1991 Gulf War, retreating Iraqi forces ignited Kuwaiti oil wells, producing dense smoke from Feb through Nov 1991. Exposure falls under 38 CFR 3.317 SW Asia and PACT Act burn-pit presumptions.
notes: No oil-fire-specific presumption; relies on 3.317 SW Asia theater and PACT Act presumptions.

description: Depleted uranium used in armor-piercing munitions and tank/vehicle armor during 1990-91 Gulf War, Bosnia, Iraq, and Afghanistan. VA operates the MEDU Surveillance Center at the Baltimore VA. Claims case-by-case.
notes: No presumptive conditions; claims case-by-case.

description: Asbestos widely used in Navy ships, insulation, roofing, flooring, cement, pipes, brake linings. High-exposure occupations: shipyard workers, demolition crews, boiler-room personnel, pipefitters.
presumptive_authority: VA recognition per https://www.va.gov/disability/eligibility/hazardous-materials-exposure/asbestos; M21-1 adjudication procedures

documentation_requirements.why: Documents MOS/rating with known asbestos exposure
Asbestos-related condition (mesothelioma, asbestosis, lung cancer, pleural plaques)
Links condition to in-service asbestos exposure

notes: Case-by-case adjudication; no presumptive conditions.

description: Military firefighters and installation personnel using AFFF since the 1970s exposed to PFAS. DoD identified 723 installations. VA currently handles PFAS claims case-by-case.
presumptive_authority: VA recognition per https://www.publichealth.va.gov/exposures/pfas.asp; no presumptive statute yet

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms firefighter or other AFFF-exposed role
723 DoD installations identified with potential PFAS release

notes: Kidney cancer under VA PACT presumptive review.

exposure_name: Edgewood Arsenal / Aberdeen Chemical Warfare Testing
description: From 1955 to 1975, Army Chemical Corps conducted classified medical studies at Edgewood Arsenal, MD. ~7,000 soldier-volunteers exposed to 250+ chemicals (sarin, VX, BZ, LSD, PCP, etc).

documentation_requirements.why: Documents participation in specific chemical study

notes: PACT Act mandated additional study.

description: Service at Fort McClellan, AL 1935-1999 may have involved radioactive Cs-137/Co-60, chemical warfare decon training, fog oil, HC smoke, and off-post PCB contamination from Monsanto Anniston.
notes: PACT Act mandates epidemiological study.

description: Trichloroethylene groundwater contamination at non-Lejeune military installations (EPA Superfund authority, case-by-case VA, no 38 CFR 3.307 presumption).
presumptive_authority: EPA Superfund / VA case-by-case under 38 CFR 3.303
notes: Distinct from Camp Lejeune presumption; individual claim development required.

description: Tetrachloroethylene (perchloroethylene) groundwater contamination at non-Lejeune military installations.
presumptive_authority: EPA Superfund / VA case-by-case under 38 CFR 3.303
notes: Distinct from Camp Lejeune presumption; individual claim development required.

exposure_name: Chemical Warfare Agents - Manufacturing / Storage / Decon Training
description: Chemical warfare agent (sarin, VX, mustard) manufacturing, storage, or decontamination training exposure outside the 38 CFR 3.316 full-body testing presumption. Includes Rocky Mountain Arsenal sarin/VX production 1950-1952 and Aberdeen storage.
notes: Distinct from 38 CFR 3.316 (which covers WWII full-body human-test volunteers).

description: PCB exposure from industrial / training environments (e.g., Fort McClellan PCBs from adjacent Monsanto Anniston plant 1929-1971; Aberdeen, Lowry landfill).
notes: Fort McClellan PCBs from adjacent Monsanto Anniston plant 1929-1971; Aberdeen, Lowry landfill, others.

exposure_name: NAF Atsugi - Jinkanpo Incinerator Dioxin / Benzene Exposure
description: NAF Atsugi exposure to dioxin and benzene from off-base Jinkanpo/Shinkampo Incinerator 1985-2001.
notes: Case-by-case; not a 38 CFR 3.309 presumption.

description: Biological agents from research/testing facilities (e.g., Fort Detrick Area B - B. globigii, S. marcescens, anthrax simulants, phosgene, radiological tracers, 1955-1970).
JP-4, JP-5, JP-8 jet fuel exposure for POL specialists, aircraft fuelers, mechanics, boom operators.
notes: ATSDR Toxicological Profile TP-121 (Mar 2015) identifies military fuelers, aircraft mechanics, pilots, ground crew as primary occupational-exposure groups.

description: Naval shipboard asbestos (TSI, boiler lagging, gaskets, packing) - pre-1980 Navy steam-plant ships.
presumptive_authority: VA Asbestos (va.gov/disability/eligibility/hazardous-materials-exposure/asbestos) + M21-1 Pt VIII iii Ch.7 Sec C

documentation_requirements.why: Mesothelioma, asbestosis, lung cancer, pleural plaques

notes: M21-1 highly-probable Navy ratings: BT, MM, HT, DC, WT, FN, FC, FT, IM, FP, SO, ST, SoM, UT.

description: Brake/clutch friction product asbestos exposure (aircraft, tracked vehicles, automotive).

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms mechanic / crew chief / wheel shop duty

notes: Pre-1989 EPA partial brake-asbestos ban; service-wide DoD phase-out by early 1990s.

exposure_name: Hexavalent Chromium - Aircraft Painting / Welding / Corrosion Control
description: Cr(VI) in aircraft primer painting, welding, corrosion control.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms paint shop / welder / corrosion-control role

notes: Documented carcinogen; lung cancer and nasal/respiratory effects.

description: Occupational ionizing radiation outside 38 CFR 3.309(d) atomic-veteran presumption (shipyard radiological work, nuclear submarine engineering plants, radar/nuclear weapons technicians).
presumptive_authority: 38 CFR 3.311 (individual claim development for radiogenic diseases)
notes: Distinct from 38 CFR 3.309(d) atomic-veteran presumption; requires individual dose reconstruction and probability-of-causation analysis.

description: Isocyanate exposure from aircraft polyurethane primer/topcoat painting.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms aircraft painter / corrosion control role

notes: Documented respiratory sensitizer; aircraft corrosion-control / painter MOS.

description: Lead solder exposure for electronics technicians, avionics work.
Radiofrequency / microwave radiation from radar, communications, and EW systems.

documentation_requirements.why: Confirms radar / comm / EW system operator-maintainer role

notes: Distinct from ionizing radiation; non-ionizing exposure.

exposure_name: Diesel Generator Exhaust / Field-Power Combustion Byproducts
description: Inhalation exposure to diesel engine exhaust from tactical generators (MEP-series, AMMPS), HMMWV / FMTV / HEMTT engines, ship engine rooms, motor pool idling, and confined-space maintenance. Contains particulate matter (PM2.5), polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs), nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, formaldehyde, acrolein, and benzene. Diesel exhaust is a Group 1 carcinogen (IARC, 2012) for lung cancer; emerging links to bladder cancer.
presumptive_authority: IARC Group 1 (Diesel Engine Exhaust, 2012 monograph); NIOSH OEL; VA case-by-case for non-presumptive claims; PACT Act covers "airborne hazards" presumption only when combined with deployment-location triggers.
documentation_requirements: Service treatment records noting respiratory complaints in field/deployed environment
Duty position evidence: tactical comm site logs, motor pool assignments, generator-mechanic billet
Buddy statements describing exhaust exposure (idling generators, enclosed CP/COC spaces)
Civilian respiratory or oncology workup post-service
Sleep study if obstructive sleep apnea is being claimed secondary
notes: Note for tactical comm / cyber transport / ATC / TOC personnel: field deployments place comm gear inside or next to tactical generators (MEP-803/806/831, AMMPS) that run continuously. Combined RF + diesel exhaust exposure is a real signature for these jobs and is under-claimed because the cyber/comm AFSC titles read "office work" to raters. Buddy statements are critical.
